threshold_count: Disturbance in attention and awareness PLUS at least one additional cognitive deficit
duration_rule: Short period (hours to a few days); symptoms typically fluctuate in severity

symptoms.description: The patient has evidence from their history, physical examination, or laboratory findings that the disturbance is a direct physiological consequence of another medical condition, substance intoxication or withdrawal, or toxin exposure.
The patient exhibits reduced awareness of the environment and difficulty directing, focusing, sustaining, or shifting attention.
The patient exhibits reduced orientation or awareness of their environment.
The patient exhibits a memory deficit, particularly difficulty learning new material or recalling recent events.
The patient experiences disorientation to time, place, or person.
The patient exhibits a language disturbance, such as rambling, disjointed speech, or difficulty finding words.
The patient exhibits a visuospatial deficit, particularly in navigating environments.
The patient exhibits a perceptual deficit, such as misinterpreting stimuli or experiencing hallucinations.

threshold_count: Evidence of significant decline in 1 or more domains; scores 2+ standard deviations below norm; interferes with independence

symptoms.description: The patient shows evidence of a significant cognitive decline from a previous level of performance in one or more cognitive domains.
The patient has cognitive decline that is documented by standardized neuropsychological testing or another quantified clinical assessment.
The patient experiences cognitive deficits that are severe enough to interfere with their independence in everyday activities.
The patient exhibits symptoms that are not better explained by delirium.

threshold_count: Evidence of modest decline in 1 or more domains; scores 1–2 standard deviations below norm; does not materialsly impair independence

symptoms.description: The patient shows evidence of a modest cognitive decline from a previous level of performance in one or more cognitive domains.
The patient demonstrates cognitive performance that is 1 to 2 standard deviations below the norm on standardized testing.
The patient has cognitive deficits that do not materially impair their independence in daily activities.
The patient exhibits symptoms that are not better explained by delirium.

diagnosis_name: Neurocognitive Disorder Due to Alzheimer’s Disease
threshold_count: Meets NCD criteria PLUS insidious onset and gradual progression in 2+ domains (major) or 1+ (mild)

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits an insidious onset of cognitive decline.
The patient demonstrates a steady, gradual progression of cognitive decline.
The patient exhibits symptoms that are not better explained by another neurocognitive etiology.

threshold_count: Meets NCD criteria PLUS onset related to 1 or more cerebrovascular events
duration_rule: Chronic; often follows a stepwise progression

symptoms.description: The patient experiences an onset of cognitive decline related to one or more cerebrovascular events.
The patient exhibits a stepwise progression of cognitive decline.
The patient exhibits a prominent decline in frontal or executive functioning, such as difficulty planning, organizing, or making decisions.
The patient exhibits reduced awareness of the environment and difficulty directing, focusing, sustaining, or shifting attention.

diagnosis_name: Neurocognitive Disorder Due to Traumatic Brain Injury
threshold_count: Meets NCD criteria PLUS evidence of TBI with immediate post-injury symptoms (LOC, amnesia, etc.)

symptoms.description: The patient has evidence of head trauma with rapid movement or displacement of the brain within the skull.
The patient loses consciousness immediately following a traumatic head injury.
The patient experiences amnesia for the traumatic event that caused the brain injury.
The patient exhibits neurological signs, such as seizures, loss of smell, or hemiparesis, following a traumatic brain injury.
